content:
===BEGIN===
Have you ever noticed how it gets really hot in the summer and cold in the winter? That happens because of the way Earth moves around the Sun! Earth is like a big ball that spins around slowly, but it’s also tilted a little bit—like when you lean to one side in your chair.

Because Earth is tilted, different parts of our planet get more sunlight at different times of the year. When the top half of Earth (called the Northern Hemisphere) leans toward the Sun, it gets lots of sunlight and it’s summer there. When it leans away from the Sun, it gets less sunlight and it’s winter.

So, the seasons change because Earth’s tilt makes different places get more or less sunlight as Earth moves around the Sun. That’s why we have spring, summer, fall, and winter—pretty cool, right?
===END===
